notes: muP base-width sweep (train_harness.py, SWEEP_LR env var). Architecture matches A100 notebook's ratios (n_states=64, branch_dim=d_model/2) so the swept LR is valid to transfer via lr*(d_model_base/d_model).

Chosen as BASE_LR for push4 -- diminishing returns visibly start here (next doubling only bought 0.009 bpb), and a much bigger/deeper model is assumed to have less LR headroom than this 6.3M-param proxy, not more.

Extra point past the grid to confirm the curve was actually flattening, not just at the edge of an untested range (same mistake exp6 made in the original local sweep, stopping right where gains were still nonzero). No divergence/instability at this LR either.
